title: Rubus gravetii
intro: Rubus gravetii, also known as Wild Raspberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its leaves, stems, and roots offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rubus gravetii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Rubus gravetii is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used to make infusions, salves, and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Rubus gravetii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family.
